skill.name: agentdb-learning-plugins
skill.description: Create and train AI learning plugins with AgentDB's 9 reinforcement learning algorithms. Includes Decision Transformer, Q-Learning, SARSA, Actor-Critic, and more. Use when building self-learning agents, implementing RL, or optimizing agent behavior through experience.
skill.summary: Build AgentDB learning plugins for reinforcement learning agents.
skill.author: ruvnet

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command and blocker patterns, but review shows most are Markdown fences, TypeScript examples, RL terminology, and public documentation links. The real risk is supply-chain exposure from repeated npx agentdb@latest command examples and local persistence in an AgentDB SQLite file, so publication is acceptable with a warning.

medium_findings.title: Package Manager Execution Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs users to run npx agentdb@latest commands. This is a legitimate setup pattern for the documented tool, but it executes a remote package at the latest version and creates supply-chain risk if the package or registry is compromised.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit and user-actionable, and npx with @latest can execute package code from the registry. The context appears legitimate documentation, so this is a warning-level risk rather than evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence False Positives
low_findings.description: The static analyzer classified many Markdown code fences and example TypeScript or JSON blocks as Ruby backtick execution. These snippets are documentation examples and are not executable skill code by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced ranges are fenced Markdown examples, not Ruby source files or runtime hooks. I did not find evidence that the skill automatically executes these snippets.

low_findings.title: Public Documentation Links
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URLs point to the AgentDB GitHub project and website. They do not include credentials, tracking parameters, or instructions to exfiltrate local data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both URLs are visible documentation references near the Learn More section. No evidence found of hidden network calls or data transfer logic.

low_findings.title: Local SQLite Persistence Example
low_findings.description: The SQLite database path is a local AgentDB example path for learning data. It may store training experiences, but it is not a credential file and is not sent to a remote endpoint.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line clearly creates a local learning database path, which can persist user-provided training data. The risk depends on the sensitivity of the experiences stored by the user.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Reconnaissance False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography hits correspond to reinforcement learning terms such as Q-Learning and descriptive text, not cryptographic algorithms. The reconnaissance hit is a use-case phrase about grid worlds and games, not system probing.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are natural-language RL documentation and training loop examples. No evidence found of cryptographic use, credential access, host enumeration, or prompt injection.

low_findings.title: Critical Heuristic Dismissed After Context Review
low_findings.description: The dangerous combination heuristic was triggered by command examples, public links, and a local database path. Review found no credential access, obfuscation, hidden network transfer, or malicious control flow.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious signals are explainable from the skill purpose and documentation format. The remaining verified issue is supply-chain execution guidance, not confirmed malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Unpinned npx Package Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: Examples use npx agentdb@latest instead of a pinned package version. Users should verify the package source or pin a trusted version before running commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact package-manager commands are present in user-facing examples. The risk is inherent to executing remote package code, even though the skill intent appears legitimate.

content.value_statement: Reinforcement learning agents need structured experience storage and training workflows. This skill guides AgentDB plugin creation, algorithm selection, training, and evaluation.
actual_capabilities: Explains how to create AgentDB learning plugins from CLI templates.
Covers nine reinforcement learning approaches, including Decision Transformer and Q-Learning.
Shows how to store training experiences in AgentDB patterns.
Describes training, evaluation, replay, and incremental learning workflows.
Provides troubleshooting guidance for convergence, overfitting, and slow training.
limitations: Requires Node.js and the external AgentDB package to run examples.
Does not include a complete production agent implementation.
Assumes users understand reinforcement learning concepts and validation metrics.
Training results depend on data quality, environment design, and package behavior.

use_cases.title: Prototype a Learning Agent
use_cases.description: Create a template plugin, collect experiences, and train an initial model for a controlled task.

use_cases.title: Compare RL Algorithms
use_cases.description: Select between Decision Transformer, Q-Learning, SARSA, Actor-Critic, and related approaches for a project.

use_cases.title: Add Learning to Reasoning Workflows
use_cases.description: Combine stored experiences with reasoning retrieval to improve decisions over repeated agent runs.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Template
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose an AgentDB learning plugin template for a discrete decision task with logged training examples.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Training Data
prompt_templates.prompt: Design an AgentDB experience collection workflow for my agent. Include state, action, reward, and evaluation fields.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Training Stability
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my reinforcement learning training plan and suggest changes for convergence, validation, and overfitting control.

prompt_templates.title: Design Multi-Agent Learning
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an AgentDB multi-agent learning strategy that separates agent experiences but trains a shared decision model.

output_examples.input: I need a learning plugin for a turn-based game with discrete actions.
output_examples.output: Recommended approach: start with Q-Learning for a simple baseline, then compare SARSA if exploration risk matters. Store each turn as state, action, reward, next state, and done status.

output_examples.input: My AgentDB model is improving in training but performs poorly on new scenarios.
output_examples.output: Likely issue: overfitting to repeated experiences. Add a validation split, reduce training epochs, diversify replay samples, and track performance on held-out episodes.

output_examples.input: I want to combine reasoning retrieval with learning history.
output_examples.output: Use AgentDB to retrieve similar successful experiences, then combine the retrieved context with a trained policy suggestion. Compare confidence before taking action.

best_practices: Pin and verify external package versions before running npx commands.
Validate reward design with small controlled experiments before long training runs.
Keep training, validation, and evaluation experiences clearly separated.
anti_patterns: Do not train on sensitive production data without a data retention plan.
Do not assume one reinforcement learning algorithm fits every action space.
Do not publish agent behavior changes without measuring held-out performance.

faq.question: What does this skill help me build?
faq.answer: It helps you create AgentDB learning plugins for agents that improve from stored experiences.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it train models by itself?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and examples. Actual training requires AgentDB, Node.js, data, and runtime execution.

faq.question: Why is there a security warning?
faq.answer: The skill includes npx commands that execute an external package. Users should verify or pin package versions.

faq.question: Can it handle private training data?
faq.answer: It can describe local storage workflows, but users must protect sensitive experiences and database files.

faq.question: Which algorithms are covered?
faq.answer: It covers Decision Transformer, Q-Learning, SARSA, Actor-Critic, Active Learning, Adversarial Training, Curriculum Learning, Federated Learning, and Multi-Task Learning.
